item.title: FirstFT: World leaders flock to China to see Xi Jinping
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Samsung workers set for $400,000 bonus, and Byju’s founder sentenced to jail in Singapore

item.title: US-backed rare earth rivals clash over alleged technology theft
item.description: Legal battle erupts between mining companies supported by Trump administration to break dependence on China

item.title: Uber adds to its Delivery Hero stake at €12bn valuation
item.description: Share purchase from Aspex Management steps up US ride-hailing group’s pursuit of German food delivery company

item.title: Uber bid for Delivery Hero would be an odd route to superapp status
item.description: Food delivery group’s taxed operating profit this year is only about 2.4 per cent of its enterprise value

item.title: For ailing Lululemon, going private might not be a stretch
item.description: The company has essentially no net debt and still generated $1bn in free cash flow last year

item.title: Preventing a ‘Chernobyl moment’ in AI
item.description: A White House order on testing frontier models would be a significant first step

item.title: EU pushes for ‘tech sovereignty’ to cut reliance on US
item.description: Draft strategy marks shift from regulating Big Tech to favouring European services

item.title: ExxonMobil wins decisive backing to move corporate domicile to Texas
item.description: Oil major to relocate incorporation from New Jersey as it seeks to parry investor activism

item.title: Robinhood to let investors use AI chatbots for share trading
item.description: US brokerage joins ‘arms race’ to give retail investors new tools

item.title: The future of consulting
item.description: Plus, why so many young people are leaving academia and the unprecedented costs of being a grandparent

item.title: Fifa subpoenaed over World Cup ticket practices in two US states
item.description: New York joins with New Jersey in probe of ‘sky-high prices’ just weeks before tournament kicks off

item.title: Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan could spend up to $20bn on a new acquisition
item.description: Bank chief executive says Trump administration’s lighter approach to regulation has freed as much as $50bn in excess capital

item.title: Et tu too, FTSE Russell?
item.description: A new telecoms value stock is coming to town

item.title: Data centre owner DigitalBridge buys energy PE firm ArcLight for $1bn
item.description: Tie-up comes as Wall Street heavyweights strike partnerships to uncover new sources of power

item.title: OpenAI’s foundation to spend $250mn on research into AI’s impact on economy
item.description: First indication of how group will deploy its cash after vowing in March to hand out $1bn over 12 months

item.title: Securitisation could help plug Europe’s funding gap
item.description: Deeper capital markets are essential to the continent’s growth

item.title: Central banks complete successful tests of cross-border blockchain payments
item.description: Prototype, backed by Federal Reserve Bank of New York and BoE, allows near-instantaneous settlement of payments

item.title: First Abu Dhabi Bank exposed to insolvencies of MFS-linked properties
item.description: UAE’s largest bank financed 60 companies now set to appoint administrators

item.title: Sotheby’s puts $30mn estimate on Tyrannosaurus rex named Gus
item.description: Record-breaking figure comes as wealthy collectors invest in fossils

item.title: Lululemon shakes up board as part of truce with founder
item.description: Athleisure brand will add two of Chip Wilson’s three picks in exchange for 18-month pause in agitations

item.title: The politics of unrealistic numbers
item.description: How to spot a load-bearing “up to”

item.title: Bolloré deals blow to Ackman’s €55bn offer for Universal Music
item.description: Chief of family group controlling nearly a third of UMG shares says bid should be rejected

item.title: Memory chipmakers ride AI boom to join trillion-dollar club
item.description: South Korea’s SK Hynix and US-based Micron bring tally of companies to achieve $1tn market capitalisation to 16

item.title: Pope’s appeal can’t change the AI race’s risky logic
item.description: Pontiff’s safety crusade runs into basic problems of game theory

item.title: Waitrose steps up competition for affluent shoppers with price cuts
item.description: Slowing grocery sales ignite battle for cost-conscious middle class

item.title: Pets at Home sales boosted by new product lines and lower prices
item.description: Turnaround plan starts to yield results in second half despite 60% fall in profits

item.title: FirstFT: Trump’s Board of Peace is stuck in limbo
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Trump-backed firebrand wins in Texas and BP’s boardroom shake-up

item.title: Six countries ask EU to shield heavy industry from carbon costs
item.description: Poland, Czech Republic and others call for more emissions permits given global crises and high energy prices

item.title: Dulux owner AkzoNobel rejects €13bn attempt to gatecrash merger
item.description: Shares jump as Nippon Paint and Sherwin-Williams attempt to muscle in on deal between Dutch group and Axalta

item.title: US regulator delays decision on AstraZeneca cancer drug
item.description: Review period extension comes after FDA oncology advisory panel voted against recommending breast cancer drug
